sentence.content: Presenter: The children’s book festival is coming up again soon and here to tell us all about it is the festival’s organiser, Jenny Morgan. So tell us what we can expect this year, Jenny.
sentence.content_zh: 主持人：儿童图书节马上又要开始了，在这里向我们做介绍的是儿童图书节的组织者珍妮·摩根。珍妮，请告诉我们今年有什么令人期待的活动。

sentence.content: Jenny: Well, as usual we’ve got five days of action-packed exciting events for children, with writers coming from all over the country getting involved.
sentence.content_zh: 珍妮：好的，和往年一样，我们为孩子们准备了五天精彩纷呈的活动，全国各地的作家都将参与其中。

sentence.content: Just to give you an idea of what’s on offer in the workshops
sentence.content_zh: 让我们来了解一下工作坊的活动内容吧。

sentence.content: First of all, there’s a very special event called Superheroes.
sentence.content_zh: 首先，我们将举办一个名为 "超级英雄"的特别活动。

sentence.content: This is a chance for deaf children to share their reading experiences with author Madeleine Gordon, who is herself hearing impaired.
sentence.content_zh: 这是一个让聋哑儿童同作家马德琳·戈登分享阅读经验的机会，她本人也有听力障碍。

sentence.content: ‘Just do it’ is a practical workshop led by the well-known illustrator Mark Keane.
sentence.content_zh: “想做就做”是一个由著名插画家马克·基恩主持的实践工作坊。

sentence.content: He’ll take participants on a magical journey to faraway lands with an opportunity for aspiring actors to do some role play.
sentence.content_zh: 他将带领参与者踏上前往远方的魔幻之旅，并为有抱负的演员提供角色扮演的机会。

sentence.content: ‘Count on me’ is an inspiring and entertaining look at the issues of friendship for 13–14-year-olds.
sentence.content_zh: 依靠我”活动将寓教于乐，带领13至14岁的青少年对友谊问题进行探究。

sentence.content: It looks at some of the friendships described in popular books and asks participants to compare these with their own experiences.
sentence.content_zh: 活动将探讨一些流行书籍中描述的友谊，并要求参与者将其与自己的亲身经历做比较。

sentence.content: ‘Speak up’ is part of a series of workshops on the subject of mental health.
sentence.content_zh: “大声说出来”是心理健康主题系列工作坊的一部分。

sentence.content: This is a creative writing workshop encouraging children to describe situations where young people experience loneliness.
sentence.content_zh: 这是一个创意写作工作坊，鼓励孩子们描述青少年经历孤独的情境。

sentence.content: A recent survey revealed that children can be lonely even when they’re at home with their families.
sentence.content_zh: 最近的一项调查显示，即使孩子们和家人在一起，他们也会感到孤独。

sentence.content: ‘Jump for joy’, as many of you will know, is the heart-warming, best-selling story by Nina Karan about a young girl’s trip to visit her relatives in India.
sentence.content_zh: “欢呼雀跃”，很多人都知道，尼娜·卡兰的畅销书《欢呼雀跃》讲述了一个小女孩去印度探亲的感人故事。

sentence.content: It recently received the gold medal at the Waterford Awards. Nina will get children to celebrate the word ‘joy’ by writing a poem.
sentence.content_zh: 该书最近获得了沃特福德奖的金奖。尼娜将让孩子们写一首诗来赞美“欢乐”这个词。

sentence.content: ‘Sticks and stones’ is the beautifully illustrated picture book for young readers about a community who organise an African-Caribbean festival to help local children learn about their Jamaican roots.
sentence.content_zh: 《棍棒和石头》是一本针对小读者的图文并茂的图画书，讲述了一个社区组织了一个非洲-加勒比文化节来帮助当地儿童了解他们的牙买加血统的故事。

sentence.content: This will be a musical event where children will have the chance to play steel drums. This is bound to be very popular, so please book as soon as possible.
sentence.content_zh: 这个文化节也将是一次音乐盛会，孩子们将有机会演奏钢鼓。这本书一定会非常受欢迎，请尽快预订。

sentence.content: Presenter: That all sounds really interesting. I’m just wondering if you have a favourite book you could recommend for our readers?
sentence.content_zh: 主持人：这一切听起来真的很有趣。我想知道您有没有一本最喜欢的书可以推荐给我们的读者？

sentence.content: Jenny: It’s hard to choose, but Alive and Kicking is definitely worth mentioning.
sentence.content_zh: 珍妮：很难选择，但《活力四射》绝对值得一提。

sentence.content: You won’t have heard of the writer as it’s her first book – which is really impressive.
sentence.content_zh: 你可能没听说过这位作家，因为这是她的第一本书-一本令人印象深刻的书。

sentence.content: It’s basically the teenage diary of a boy from Somalia who comes to live in the UK.
sentence.content_zh: 这本书记述了一个从索马里来到英国生活的少年的日记。

sentence.content: It deals with the serious issue of immigration and all the challenges the boy has to face at school and with the language barrier, etc.
sentence.content_zh: 这本书涉及移民问题这一严肃话题，以及男孩在学校适应和语言障碍等方面必须面对的挑战。

sentence.content: Usually, books like this are quite sad, but this one actually made me cry with laughter.
sentence.content_zh: 一般来说，这样的书都比较伤感，但这本书却让我笑中带泪。

sentence.content: On each page, there are simple but hilarious black and white stick drawings of the boy with his friends and teachers.
sentence.content_zh: 在每一页上，都有描绘小男孩与朋友及老师相处过程的简洁有趣的黑白简笔画。

sentence.content: At the end of each diary entry, there are new English words the boy learns each day, which may help develop some children’s vocabulary.
sentence.content_zh: 在每篇日记的末尾会有男孩每天学习的新英语单词，这可能有助于扩展一些孩子的词汇量。

sentence.content: Presenter: I think my kids would enjoy that. What about any advice for parents on how to encourage their children to read more?
sentence.content_zh: 主持人：我想我的孩子会喜欢的。对于如何鼓励孩子多读书，您对家长有什么建议呢？

sentence.content: Jenny: Well, this is something I get asked about a lot.
sentence.content_zh: 珍妮：我经常被问到这件事。

sentence.content: There are so many distractions for kids these days that it can be hard to find time for reading.
sentence.content_zh: 如今，孩子们有太多的事情要分心，很难抽出时间来阅读。

sentence.content: One thing I’d say is to make time to sit down with your child and share books with them.
sentence.content_zh: 我想说的一件事是，家长应该抽出时间和孩子坐在一起，与他们分享书籍。

sentence.content: A lot of parents give up reading aloud to their children as soon as they learn to read independently, but this is a mistake.
sentence.content_zh: 很多家长在孩子学会独立阅读后就不再朗读，其实这是错误的。

sentence.content: It’s good to read more advanced books to them as it helps to develop their vocabulary.
sentence.content_zh: 为他们朗读更高级的书籍很有好处，因为这有助于扩展他们的词汇量。

sentence.content: If you don’t have time for this, then let them listen to audio books. Often, they’ll want to read books they’ve listened to for themselves.
sentence.content_zh: 如果您没有时间，可以让他们听有声读物。通常，他们会想自己阅读已经听过的书。

sentence.content: I think it’s a good idea to make a mental note of the type of books your child is reading – often they just read the same genre all the time, which can get a bit boring.
sentence.content_zh: 我认为，最好在头脑中记下您孩子正在阅读的书籍类型-他们往往总是阅读同一类型的书籍，这可能会让他们感到有些乏味。

sentence.content: You can introduce new authors and genres to them. Librarians should be able to help you with this.
sentence.content_zh: 家长可以向孩子们推荐新的作者和书籍类型。图书管理员应该能在这方面帮到家长们。

sentence.content: Presenter: Well Jenny, I think that’s really useful. . . .
sentence.content_zh: 主持人：珍妮，我觉得这些建议真的很有用……

added_content.ex_information:
Choose
TWO
letters,
A–E
.

obj.content: Which [b]TWO[/b] reasons does the speaker give for recommending Alive and Kicking?
obj.html_content: Which TWO reasons does the speaker give for recommending Alive and Kicking?

obj.content: It will appeal to both boys and girls.
obj.html_content: It will appeal to both boys and girls.

obj.content: The author is well known.
obj.html_content: The author is well known.

obj.content: It has colourful illustrations.
obj.html_content: It has colourful illustrations.

obj.content: It is funny.
obj.html_content: It is funny.

obj.content: It deals with an important topic.
obj.html_content: It deals with an important topic.

sentence.content_zh: “欢呼雀跃”，很多人都知道，尼娜·卡兰的畅销书《欢呼雀跃》讲述了一个小女孩去印度探亲的感人故事。

sentence.content: It recently received the gold medal at the Waterford Awards. Nina will get children to celebrate the word ‘joy’ by writing a poem.
sentence.content_zh: 该书最近获得了沃特福德奖的金奖。尼娜将让孩子们写一首诗来赞美“欢乐”这个词。

sentence.content: ‘Sticks and stones’ is the beautifully illustrated picture book for young readers about a community who organise an African-Caribbean festival to help local children learn about their Jamaican roots.
sentence.content_zh: 《棍棒和石头》是一本针对小读者的图文并茂的图画书，讲述了一个社区组织了一个非洲-加勒比文化节来帮助当地儿童了解他们的牙买加血统的故事。

sentence.content: This will be a musical event where children will have the chance to play steel drums. This is bound to be very popular, so please book as soon as possible.
sentence.content_zh: 这个文化节也将是一次音乐盛会，孩子们将有机会演奏钢鼓。这本书一定会非常受欢迎，请尽快预订。

sentence.content: Presenter: That all sounds really interesting. I’m just wondering if you have a favourite book you could recommend for our readers?
sentence.content_zh: 主持人：这一切听起来真的很有趣。我想知道您有没有一本最喜欢的书可以推荐给我们的读者？

sentence.content: Jenny: It’s hard to choose, but Alive and Kicking is definitely worth mentioning.
sentence.content_zh: 珍妮：很难选择，但《活力四射》绝对值得一提。

sentence.content: You won’t have heard of the writer as it’s her first book – which is really impressive.
sentence.content_zh: 你可能没听说过这位作家，因为这是她的第一本书-一本令人印象深刻的书。

sentence.content: It’s basically the teenage diary of a boy from Somalia who comes to live in the UK.
sentence.content_zh: 这本书记述了一个从索马里来到英国生活的少年的日记。

sentence.content: It deals with the serious issue of immigration and all the challenges the boy has to face at school and with the language barrier, etc.
sentence.content_zh: 这本书涉及移民问题这一严肃话题，以及男孩在学校适应和语言障碍等方面必须面对的挑战。

sentence.content: Usually, books like this are quite sad, but this one actually made me cry with laughter.
sentence.content_zh: 一般来说，这样的书都比较伤感，但这本书却让我笑中带泪。

sentence.content: On each page, there are simple but hilarious black and white stick drawings of the boy with his friends and teachers.
sentence.content_zh: 在每一页上，都有描绘小男孩与朋友及老师相处过程的简洁有趣的黑白简笔画。

sentence.content: At the end of each diary entry, there are new English words the boy learns each day, which may help develop some children’s vocabulary.
sentence.content_zh: 在每篇日记的末尾会有男孩每天学习的新英语单词，这可能有助于扩展一些孩子的词汇量。

sentence.content: Presenter: I think my kids would enjoy that. What about any advice for parents on how to encourage their children to read more?
sentence.content_zh: 主持人：我想我的孩子会喜欢的。对于如何鼓励孩子多读书，您对家长有什么建议呢？

sentence.content: Jenny: Well, this is something I get asked about a lot.
sentence.content_zh: 珍妮：我经常被问到这件事。

sentence.content: There are so many distractions for kids these days that it can be hard to find time for reading.
sentence.content_zh: 如今，孩子们有太多的事情要分心，很难抽出时间来阅读。

sentence.content: One thing I’d say is to make time to sit down with your child and share books with them.
sentence.content_zh: 我想说的一件事是，家长应该抽出时间和孩子坐在一起，与他们分享书籍。

sentence.content: A lot of parents give up reading aloud to their children as soon as they learn to read independently, but this is a mistake.
sentence.content_zh: 很多家长在孩子学会独立阅读后就不再朗读，其实这是错误的。

sentence.content: It’s good to read more advanced books to them as it helps to develop their vocabulary.
sentence.content_zh: 为他们朗读更高级的书籍很有好处，因为这有助于扩展他们的词汇量。

sentence.content: If you don’t have time for this, then let them listen to audio books. Often, they’ll want to read books they’ve listened to for themselves.
sentence.content_zh: 如果您没有时间，可以让他们听有声读物。通常，他们会想自己阅读已经听过的书。

sentence.content: I think it’s a good idea to make a mental note of the type of books your child is reading – often they just read the same genre all the time, which can get a bit boring.
sentence.content_zh: 我认为，最好在头脑中记下您孩子正在阅读的书籍类型-他们往往总是阅读同一类型的书籍，这可能会让他们感到有些乏味。

sentence.content: You can introduce new authors and genres to them. Librarians should be able to help you with this.
sentence.content_zh: 家长可以向孩子们推荐新的作者和书籍类型。图书管理员应该能在这方面帮到家长们。

sentence.content: Presenter: Well Jenny, I think that’s really useful. . . .
sentence.content_zh: 主持人：珍妮，我觉得这些建议真的很有用……

officialExplain.explain: 题干Superheroes在原文中原词重现，大致定位。接下来的一句中，deaf对应C选项的disability，children原词重现。deaf意思是“聋的”，disability意思是“缺陷，障碍”。之后文中提及了一位作者，虽然B选项中也包括author，但此题在原文中相关内容为听力受损，与B选项不符。因此答案为C。

题干Just do it在原文中原词重现，大致定位。同一句中出现的led by虽然在B选项中也有，但此处在原文中相关内容为well-known illustrator，并非B选项中的prize-winning author。illustrator意为“插图画家”，但原文未提及画，与A选项的painting and drawing不符。之后的原文中，actors和role play对应D选项中的drama activity。因此答案为D。
题干Count on me在原文中原词重现，大致定位。接下来的内容提及friendship，看似与E选项有关，但并未表现出new relationships。原文中的13–14-year-olds表示13到14岁大的人或青少年，对应F选项中的specific age group，意思是“特定的年龄段”。因此答案为F。
题干Speak up在原文中原词重现，大致定位。文中先提及该工坊是关于mental health心理健康的，之后讲到鼓励孩子们描述年轻人经历孤独的情况。文中的名词loneliness意为“孤独，寂寞，孤单”，形容词lonely表示“孤独的，寂寞的”或“在孤单中度过的”，对应G选项中的unhappy feeling。因此答案为G。
题干Jump for joy在原文中原词重现，大致定位。原文中讲到这是一个感人的畅销故事，近期获得了Waterford Awards的金牌，对应B选项中的prize-winning author，意为“获奖作家”。因此答案为B

题干Stick and stones在原文中原词重现，大致定位。之后文中提及African-Caribbean以及Jamaican roots对应H选项中的particular culture，这里的roots可以理解为“根”，在词典中的解释为“与出生地或原籍相关联的情感或联系”。原文中的help local children learn about对应H选项中的raises awareness，raise awareness of sth.表示“提升/加强对……的意识”。因此答案为H。

问题中的关键词Alive and Kicking在原文中原词重现。接下来原文提及won’t have heard of the writer并说到这是该作者的第一本书，与B选项的well known不符，排除B。接着提到该书涉及serious issue，对应E选项中的important topic，其中的deals with原词重现。serious除了“严肃的，认真的”之外，还可以表示“重要的，须重视的”，issue表示“（有关某事的）问题”或“重要议题”。原文中的重要问题包括immigration，意为“移居（入境）”，以及这个男孩在学校面临的所有挑战。因此E为其中一个正确选项。原文在quite sad之后出现but，之后的made me cry with laughter对应D选项中的funny。因此D为正确选项。另外原文中讲到在书的每一页，都有简单但滑稽的简笔画，相关颜色为black and white，C选项中虽然提及illustrations，但colourful与原文不符合，排除C。原文中多次提及这本书中的人物，后来又讲到该书有助于扩展孩子们的词汇，presenter还说my kids would enjoy that，而A选项的appeal to both boys and girls虽然提及孩子们，但是表达的是吸引男孩们和女孩们，意思与原文不符合，排除A。因此这两题的答案为D、E。

问题中的关键词advice和parents在原文中原词重现，reading在原文中替换为read。在原文的同一句中，出现了encourage和children，与A选项部分信息一致，但并未出现A中的write down new vocabulary相关信息。原文接下来的段落中提及give up reading aloud to their children as soon as they learn to read independently，这部分信息与E选项对应，但原文中紧接着出现but，并将这种情况称为mistake，因此这并不是说话者的建议，排除E。之后原文讲到vocabulary，看似对应A选项部分信息，但此处相关内容是说给孩子们读更高阶的书籍能够帮助扩展词汇，与A选项中的其他信息不符，排除A。原文的下一句中讲到let them listen to audio books，let对应B选项的allow，them指代前文提及的children，audio books原词重现，因此B为正确选项。之后原文讲到孩子们总是读相同体裁/类型的书籍，you can introduce new authors and genres to them中的you指代家长，可见说话者的建议是由家长来引入不同作者的书籍以及新的体裁/类型，而不是D选项所说的让孩子选择，排除D。最后说话者讲到Librarians可以提供帮助，C选项的librarians原词重现，意为“图书管理员”，原文中的help you with this对应C选项的recommendations，因此C为正确答案。这两题的答案为B、C。
